text: Snack vending (rolex, chapati, samosa, mandazi, juice) is the lowest-barrier business in Uganda. A rolex stand can start with as little as UGX 100,000-200,000: flat griddle/sigiri (UGX 30,000-50,000), cooking oil, eggs, flour, cabbage, tomatoes, charcoal, and a simple table. A single rolex sells for UGX 2,000-3,000, costs about UGX 800-1,200 to make. Profit per rolex: UGX 1,000-1,800. Selling 50-100 rolexes/day = UGX 50,000-180,000 daily profit. Monthly profit: UGX 1,000,000-3,000,000 for a well-located stand. Best locations: near schools, taxi parks, construction sites, market entrances.

Expanding your food vending business: 1) Start with ONE item (e.g., rolex) and perfect it before adding others. 2) Consistency is key — same location, same time, same quality every day. Customers build habits. 3) Hygiene is your best marketing: wear gloves, keep a clean station, use proper waste disposal. 4) Track your expenses daily in a simple notebook: what you bought, what you sold, what was wasted. 5) Graduate from charcoal to gas stove for faster cooking and lower long-term costs (charcoal sigiri UGX 30,000 vs gas stove UGX 150,000 but saves 40% on fuel). 6) Once profitable, consider catering for events — weddings, office lunches, school functions.

Food vending risks and mitigations: 1) Health inspection fines — get a food handler's certificate from KCCA (UGX 50,000). 2) Rain/weather — have a simple tarpaulin cover (UGX 30,000). 3) Ingredient price spikes — buy staples (flour, oil) in bulk from Nakasero wholesale. 4) Unsold food waste — start with smaller quantities and scale up as you learn demand patterns. 5) Competition — differentiate through quality, consistency, and cleanliness, not price cutting. 6) Theft of equipment at night — pack up and take home, or invest in a lockable kiosk.
